question: If P(n, r) = 2520 and C(n, r) = 21, then what is the value of C(n + 1, r + 1)?

en.content: If P(n, r) = 2520 and C(n, r) = 21, then what is the value of C(n + 1, r + 1)?
en.explanation:
If P(n, r) = 2520 and C(n, r) = 21,

$$\therefore$$ $${}^n{p_r} = 2520$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{n!} \over {(n - r)!}} = 2520$$ ..... (i) and

$${}^n{C_r} = 21$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{n!} \over {r!(n - r)!}} = 21$$ ..... (ii)

From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

$${{2520} \over {r!}} = 21$$

$$ \Rightarrow r! = {{2520} \over {21}} = 120$$

$$ \Rightarrow r! = 5!$$

$$\therefore$$ r = 5

Putting the value of r in Eq. (i),

$${{n!} \over {(n - 5)!}} = 2520$$

$$ \Rightarrow n(n - 1)(n - 2)(n - 3)(n - 4) = 7 \times 6 \times 5 \times 4 \times 3$$

$$\therefore$$ n = 7

Now, $$C(n + 1,r + 1) = {}^{n + 1}{C_{r + 1}}$$

$$ = {}^{7 + 1}{C_{5 + 1}} = {}^8{C_6}$$

$$ = {{8!} \over {6!2!}} = {{8 \times 7} \over 2} = 28$$

question: What is the number of diagonals of an octagon?

en.content: What is the number of diagonals of an octagon?
en.explanation:
The number of vertices of an octagon = 8

$$\therefore$$ The number of points in a plane = 8

$$\therefore$$ Total number of straight line form by 8 points = $${}^8{C_2}$$ [$$\because$$ 1 straight line form by 2 points]

= $${{8!} \over {2!6!}} = {{8 \times 7} \over 2} = 28$$

$$\therefore$$ The number of diagonals of an octagon = Total number of straight line form by 8 points $$-$$ number of sides of octagon = 28 $$-$$ 8 = 20

question: If C(20, n + 2) = C(20, n $$-$$2), then what is n equal to?

en.content: If C(20, n + 2) = C(20, n $$-$$2), then what is n equal to?
en.explanation:
We have, C(20, n + 2) = C(20, n $$-$$ 2)

$$ \Rightarrow {}^{20}{C_{n + 2}} = {}^{20}{C_{n - 2}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow n + 2 + n - 2 = 20$$ [$$\because$$ $${}^n{C_x} = {}^n{C_y} \Rightarrow x + y = n$$]

$$\therefore$$ n = 10

question: A tea party is arranged for 16 people along two sides of a long table with eight chairs on each side. Four particular men wish to sit on one particular side and two particular men on the other side. The number of ways they can be seated is

en.content: A tea party is arranged for 16 people along two sides of a long table with eight chairs on each side. Four particular men wish to sit on one particular side and two particular men on the other side. The number of ways they can be seated is
en.explanation:
Since, four particular men want to sit on a particular side A (say) and two other particular men on the other side B. So, we are left with 10 guests out of which we choose 4 for side A and 6 for ide B. Hence, the number of selections for the two sides = $${}^{10}{C_4}$$ $$\times$$ $${}^{6}{C_6}$$

Now, 8 persons on each side of the table can be arranged among themselves in 8! ways.

Hence, the total number of arrangements

$$ = {}^{10}{C_4} \times {}^6{C_6} \times 8!\, \times 8!$$

$$ = {{10!} \over {4!6!}} \times 1 \times 8!\, \times 8!$$

$$ = {{10 \times 9 \times 8 \times 7} \over {4 \times 3 \times 2 \times 1}} \times {(8!)^2} = 210 \times {(8!)^2}$$

question: Out of 15 points in a plane, n points are in the same straight line. 445 triangles can be formed by joining these points. What is the value of n?

en.content: Out of 15 points in a plane, n points are in the same straight line. 445 triangles can be formed by joining these points. What is the value of n?
en.explanation:
We know that,

If n distinct points in a plane, in which m are collinear (m $$\ge$$ 3), then the number of triangle formed = $${}^n{C_3}$$ $$-$$ $${}^m{C_3}$$

From question,

There are 15 points in a plane in which n points are in same straight line, then

$${}^{15}{C_3}$$ $$-$$ $${}^{n}{C_3}$$ = 445

455 $$-$$ $${}^{n}{C_3}$$ = 445

$$\Rightarrow$$ $${}^{n}{C_3}$$ = 455 $$-$$ 445 = 10

$$\Rightarrow$$ $${{n!} \over {3!(n - 3)!}} = 10$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ $${{n(n - 1)(n - 2)(n - 3)!} \over {(n - 3)!}} = 10 \times 3!$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ n(n $$-$$ 1) (n $$-$$ 2) = 60

$$\Rightarrow$$ n(n $$-$$ 1) (n $$-$$ 2) = 5 $$\times$$ 4 $$\times$$ 3

$$\therefore$$ n = 5

question: A polygon has 44 diagonals. The number of its sides is

en.content: A polygon has 44 diagonals. The number of its sides is
en.explanation:
Let the number of sides of the polygon be n.

Then, number of diagonals = $${}^n{C_2} - n$$

$$44 = {}^n{C_2} - n$$

$$ \Rightarrow 44 = {{n!} \over {2!(n - 2)!}} - n$$

$$ \Rightarrow 44 = {{n(n - 1)(n - 2)!} \over {2!(n - 2)!}} - n$$

$$ \Rightarrow 44 = {{[n(n - 1)]} \over 2} - n \Rightarrow 88 = {n^2} - n - 2n$$

$${n^2} - 3n - 88 = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow (n - 11)(n + 8) = 0 \Rightarrow n = 11$$ and $$-$$ 8

Hence, polygon has 11 sides.
